_meta.title: Jit Compilation V8 Jvm Luajit Principles

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of tiered compilation in a JIT system?
options: To compile all code to native machine code before execution
To spend compile time only on frequently-executed (hot) functions, keeping cold code cheap to interpret
To distribute compilation across multiple CPU cores
To cache compiled code to disk for faster startup on subsequent runs
questions.explanation: Tiered compilation starts with fast interpretation, then compiles only hot functions through progressively more aggressive optimization tiers. This avoids wasting time compiling code that runs infrequently.

questions.question: What happens when a monomorphic inline cache encounters a new object shape that it has not seen before?
options: The cache is cleared and the property lookup is performed from scratch
The cache is upgraded to polymorphic, storing the new shape-offset pair alongside the existing one
The JIT compiler deoptimizes the function and returns to the interpreter
A runtime exception is thrown because the shape is unexpected
questions.explanation: A monomorphic inline cache stores one shape. When a new shape is encountered, the cache upgrades to polymorphic (up to ~4 entries), allowing multiple shapes to share the call site without a full hash lookup.

questions.question: What problem do hidden classes (shapes) solve in V8?
options: They hide private properties from external code access
They allow objects with the same property layout to share property offsets, enabling fast inline-cached access
They compress object memory by removing unused property slots
They prevent prototype pollution attacks in JavaScript
questions.explanation: JavaScript objects are dynamic and can have arbitrary properties. Hidden classes assign a shared shape to objects with the same property names in the same order, so inline caches can compare a single shape pointer rather than inspecting each object's structure.

questions.question: What is on-stack replacement (OSR) and when is it useful?
options: A technique to replace the JIT compiler's intermediate representation with native code during compilation
Replacing an interpreted stack frame with a compiled one while a hot loop is executing, useful when the containing function is rarely called
Swapping the call stack between threads to balance load across CPU cores
A debugging technique that replaces optimized code with unoptimized code to preserve variable values
questions.explanation: OSR handles the case where a hot loop runs inside a function that has been called only once. Since invocation-based triggering would never fire, OSR detects hot back-edges and replaces the interpreted frame with a compiled frame mid-execution.

questions.question: What is Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation?
options: Compiling code before distribution
Compiling code to machine code at runtime, often after interpreting it and identifying hot paths
Compiling code incrementally during editing
Compiling code using multiple threads
questions.explanation: JIT compilation starts with interpretation, profiles which code runs frequently (hot paths), then compiles those to optimized machine code at runtime. This combines interpreter startup speed with compiled code performance. V8, JVM HotSpot, and LuaJIT all use this approach.
